pr.title: feat: add distributed tracing support via OpenTelemetry
pr.author: dev-rodrigo
pr.description: Integrates OpenTelemetry SDK for distributed tracing across all service boundaries. Adds span creation, context propagation, and exporter configuration. This is a foundational change that other teams will build on.

diff_summary.patch_summary: New TracerProvider wrapper with auto-instrumentation hooks and configurable sampler. Exposes startSpan, withSpan, and propagateContext helpers.
OTLP HTTP and gRPC exporter factory. Reads endpoint and auth token from environment variables OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT and OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_HEADERS.
Re-exports public API surface for telemetry module.
Express/Fastify-compatible middleware that creates a root span per incoming HTTP request and attaches trace-id to response headers.
Added telemetry config block with serviceName, serviceVersion, tracesSampleRate, and exporterType fields. Breaks existing config shape — consumers must update their config files.
Injects W3C traceparent header on outbound requests when an active span exists. Removes legacy X-Request-ID propagation logic.
Wraps each query execution in a child span with db.statement and db.operation attributes. Uses AsyncLocalStorage to retrieve parent context.
Extracts trace context from message metadata headers before processing. Falls back to a new root span when no context is present.
New observability guide covering tracer setup, sampler configuration, and exporter options with example environment variable configurations.

comments.author: dev-priya
comments.body: The config breaking change concerns me. Do we have a migration guide or a compatibility shim for existing deployments? Some teams are on a quarterly release cycle and can't update configs immediately.

comments.author: dev-rodrigo
comments.body: Fair point. I can add a compatibility layer that reads the old config shape and emits a deprecation warning. Would that unblock teams?

comments.author: dev-priya
comments.body: Yes, a deprecation shim would work for me. Let's also make sure the migration path is documented in the breaking change notes.

comments.author: dev-marcus
comments.body: What's the performance overhead of wrapping every DB query in a span? Have you benchmarked this? Concerned about latency regression on high-throughput query paths.

comments.author: dev-rodrigo
comments.body: I ran a quick benchmark locally — overhead was ~0.3ms per query at p99. But that was on my machine with the OTLP exporter disabled. Need to test with a real collector in the loop.

comments.author: dev-marcus
comments.body: We should have CI benchmark results before merging something that touches every query. Can you add a benchmark job to the pipeline?

comments.author: dev-yuki
comments.body: Removing X-Request-ID propagation in http/client.ts is a breaking change for any consumers parsing that header on the receiving end. Should we keep it for a deprecation window?

comments.author: dev-rodrigo
comments.body: Good catch. I'll restore X-Request-ID propagation alongside traceparent for now and open a separate issue to deprecate it properly.

comments.author: dev-priya
comments.body: Overall direction is great. The OTel SDK choice is solid. Main blockers for me: (1) config compat shim, (2) X-Request-ID restored. Happy to re-review once those are addressed.

comments.author: dev-marcus
comments.body: Same from me — need CI benchmarks before I can approve. The change is architecturally sound but I want data on the query overhead before we land this.

reviews.author: dev-priya
reviews.body: Needs config compat shim and migration docs before merge.

reviews.author: dev-marcus
reviews.body: Benchmark data required for db/query-runner changes.

reviews.author: dev-yuki
reviews.body: X-Request-ID removal needs a deprecation window.

expected_recommendation_highlights: Architectural direction is sound and OTel SDK choice is appropriate
Three concrete blockers identified by reviewers must be resolved before merge
Config breaking change requires a compatibility shim and migration documentation
X-Request-ID removal should be restored with a deprecation path
CI benchmark job needed to validate DB query overhead before landing
Author has acknowledged all concerns and proposed resolutions — needs follow-through
